content.problemStatement:
Chef has an integer array **X**, and a binary array **S**, both of size **N**. He wants to know how many vectors (denote by **V**) of **N** elements exist, for which the following conditions hold for all valid indices **i** (that is, for all 1 ≤ **i** ≤ **N**).


0 ≤ **Vi** < 1018If **Si** = 1, **Vi** is ≥ **Xi**, and if **Si** = 0, **Vi** ≤ **Xi**Sum of the elements of **V**, when viewed as a string, contains "47" as a substring.


Please help Chef find this count. Since it may be a very large number, you should output the count modulo **109+7**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer N, the size of the arrays X and S. The next N lines contain two integers each, Si and Xi.
content.outputFormat: The number of vectors V that satisfy the given conditions, modulo 10^9 + 7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are 6 vectors that satisfy the conditions: (17, 30), (18, 29), (19, 28), (20, 27), (21, 26), (22, 25).
There are 12 vectors that satisfy the conditions.

solution.approach: We will use a recursive approach with memoization to solve this problem. We will generate all possible vectors V and check if they satisfy the given conditions. We will use a bitmask to represent the current state of the vector V.
solution.timeComplexity: O(2^N * 10^N) — because we are generating all possible vectors V and checking each one.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(2^N) — because we are using a bitmask to represent the current state of the vector V.
